back_translation: Then Joshua called for the Reubenite and the Gadite and the half-tribe of Manasseh.
And he said to them, 'You have kept all that Moses the servant of the LORD commanded you, and you have obeyed my voice in all that I commanded you.
You have not abandoned your brothers these many days until this day, and you have kept the charge of the commandment of the LORD your God.

And now the LORD your God has given rest to your brothers, just as He spoke to them. So now, turn and go to your tents, to the land of your possession which Moses the servant of the LORD gave you across the Jordan.

Only, be very careful to do the commandment and the law which Moses the servant of the LORD commanded you, to love the LORD your God and to walk in all His ways, and to keep His commandments and to hold fast to Him, and to serve Him with all your heart and with all your soul.'
And Joshua blessed them and sent them away, and they went to their tents.

Now to the half-tribe of Manasseh Moses had given in Bashan, and to the other half Joshua gave with their brothers across the Jordan to the west. So when Joshua sent them to their tents, he blessed them.

And he said to them, saying, 'With great wealth return to your tents, and with very many livestock, with silver and with gold and with bronze and with iron and with very many clothes. Divide the spoil of your enemies with your brothers.'

So the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh returned and went from the sons of Israel at Shiloh which is in the land of Canaan, to go to the land of Gilead, to the land of their possession in which they had received possession according to the mouth (command) of the LORD by the hand of Moses.

And they came to Geliloth of the Jordan, which is in the land of Canaan, and the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh built there an altar by the Jordan — a great altar for sight (imposing).

And the sons of Israel heard, saying, 'Behold, the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh have built the altar facing the land of Canaan, at Geliloth of the Jordan, on the side belonging to the sons of Israel.'
When the sons of Israel heard, the whole congregation of the sons of Israel assembled at Shiloh to go up against them for war.
And the sons of Israel sent to the sons of Reuben and to the sons of Gad and to the half-tribe of Manasseh in the land of Gilead — Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest.
And with him ten chiefs — one chief, one chief for the house of the father for all the tribes of Israel; and a man head of the house of their fathers were they for the thousands of Israel.
And they came to the sons of Reuben and to the sons of Gad and to the half-tribe of Manasseh in the land of Gilead and spoke with them, saying,

'Thus says the whole congregation of the LORD: What is this breach of faith which you have committed against the God of Israel — to turn today from after the LORD by building for yourselves an altar to rebel today against the LORD?
Is the sin of Peor not enough for us, from which we have not cleansed ourselves to this day, and there was a plague in the congregation of the LORD?
And you would turn today from after the LORD? And it shall be — you rebel today against the LORD, and tomorrow against the whole congregation of Israel He will be angry.

And yet, if the land of your possession is unclean, cross over for yourselves to the land of the possession of the LORD where the tabernacle of the LORD dwells, and take possession in our midst. But against the LORD do not rebel, and against us do not rebel, by building for yourselves an altar besides the altar of the LORD our God.

Did not Achan son of Zerah commit a breach of faith regarding what was devoted to destruction, and wrath fell upon the whole congregation of Israel? And he was one man — he did not perish alone in his sin!'
Then the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh answered, and they spoke to the heads of the thousands of Israel:
'El, Elohim, the LORD! El, Elohim, the LORD! He knows, and Israel also will know! If it was in rebellion or in breach of faith against the LORD, do not save us this day.

To build for ourselves an altar to turn away from after the LORD, or if to offer up on it burnt offering and grain offering, or if to make on it sacrifices of peace offerings — may the LORD Himself require it.
And if not, out of anxiety from the matter we did this, saying: Tomorrow your sons might say to our sons, saying: "What have you to do with the LORD, the God of Israel?
For the LORD has placed a border between us and you, sons of Reuben and sons of Gad — the Jordan. You have no share in the LORD!" And your sons might cause our sons to cease from fearing the LORD.
So we said, 'Let us indeed take action for ourselves to build the altar — not for burnt offering and not for sacrifice.

For it is a witness between us and between you and between our generations after us, to perform the service of the LORD before Him with our burnt offerings and with our sacrifices and with our peace offerings; and your sons shall not say tomorrow to our sons, "You have no share in the LORD."'

And we said, 'And it shall be — when they say to us or to our generations tomorrow, then we will say: See the pattern of the altar of the LORD that our fathers made — not for burnt offering and not for sacrifice — but it is a witness between us and between you.'

Far be it from us, from rebelling against the LORD and from turning today from after the LORD by building an altar for burnt offering, for grain offering, and for sacrifice, apart from the altar of the LORD our God which is before His tabernacle.'

When Phinehas the priest and the chiefs of the congregation and the heads of the thousands of Israel who were with him heard the words which the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the sons of Manasseh had spoken, it was good in their eyes.

And Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest said to the sons of Reuben and to the sons of Gad and to the sons of Manasseh, 'Today we know that the LORD is in our midst, in that you have not committed this breach of faith against the LORD. Then you have delivered the sons of Israel from the hand of the LORD.'

And Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest and the chiefs returned from the sons of Reuben and from the sons of Gad, from the land of Gilead to the land of Canaan, to the sons of Israel, and they brought back a word/report.

And the word was good in the eyes of the sons of Israel, and the sons of Israel blessed God, and they did not speak of going up against them for war, to destroy the land in which the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad dwelt.
And the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad called the altar [Witness], for 'It is a witness between us that the LORD is God.'
